name: Dicloxacillin
description: One of the penicillins which is resistant to penicillinase. [PubChem]
indication: Used to treat infections caused by penicillinase-producing staphylococci which have demonstrated susceptibility to the drug.
toxicity: Oral LD<sub>50</sub> in rat is 3579 mg/kg. Symptoms of overexposure include irritation, rash, labored breathing, hives, itching, wheezing, nausea, chills, and fever.
mechanismOfAction: Dicloxacillin exerts a bactericidal action against penicillin-susceptible microorganisms during the state of active multiplication. All penicillins inhibit the biosynthesis of the bacterial cell wall. By binding to specific penicillin-binding proteins (PBPs) located inside the bacterial cell wall, dicloxacillin inhibits the third and last stage of bacterial cell wall synthesis. Cell lysis is then mediated by bacterial cell wall autolytic enzymes such as autolysins; it is possible that dicloxacillin interferes with an autolysin inhibitor.
pharmacodynamics: Dicloxacillin is a beta-lactamase resistant penicillin similar to oxacillin. Dicloxacillin has in vitro activity against gram-positive and gram-negative aerobic and anaerobic bacteria. The bactericidal activity of dicloxacillin results from the inhibition of cell wall synthesis and is mediated through dicloxacillin binding to penicillin binding proteins (PBPs). Dicloxacillin is stable against hydrolysis by a variety of beta-lactamases, including penicillinases, and cephalosporinases and extended spectrum beta-lactamases.

interactions.name: Probenecid
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Dicloxacillin can be increased when it is combined with Probenecid.

interactions.name: Rilpivirine
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Rilpivirine can be decreased when it is combined with Dicloxacillin.

interactions.name: BCG vaccine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of BCG vaccine can be decreased when used in combination with Dicloxacillin.

interactions.name: Aripiprazole
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Aripiprazole can be decreased when it is combined with Dicloxacillin.

interactions.name: Acemetacin
interactions.description: Acemetacin may decrease the excretion rate of Dicloxacillin which could result in a higher serum level.

interactions.name: Picosulfuric acid
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Picosulfuric acid can be decreased when used in combination with Dicloxacillin.

interactions.name: Doxycycline
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Dicloxacillin can be decreased when used in combination with Doxycycline.
